room_choice.text: The staff leads you to the hallway. There are three doors, each with a handwritten list of names taped to it. You have to choose where to sleep. This choice will determine who you spend your nights with for the rest of the show.

choices.text: The 'Playful' Room (Roommates: Rio & Coco).
The 'Serious' Room (Roommates: Jiwon & Kang).
The 'Peaceful' Room (Roommates: Haneul & Yuri).

room_playful_night1.text: You hear loud bass thumping from inside before you even open the door. Rio is already jumping on the bed, and Coco is trying to set up a mini-basketball hoop on the doorframe. "Party room!" Rio yells, high-fiving you. "No sleep allowed!"

room_playful_night12.text:
You walk into a room. The vibe screams focus. Kang is already unpacking his vitamins in alphabetical order. Jiwon is reading the rulebook with a highlighter.

"We are here to win," Kang says, nodding at you. "Lights out at 10 PM sharp."

room_playful_night13.text: You open the door to pure silence. Haneul is meditating in the corner. Yuri is arranging a vase of dried flowers on the nightstand. "Welcome," Yuri whispers. "Please leave your shoes and your stress at the door."

villa_night1_explore.text:
The camera crews have retreated to the perimeter. For the first time, the trainees are alone in the Villa. And honestly? It's insane.

It's not a prison. It's a playground. The kitchen is stocked with food you can't afford, the furniture costs more than your parents' car, and the air conditioning smells like lavender.

Most trainees are running around touching things, checking if they're real.

choices.text: Kang is by the pool table, lining up a shot alone.
Jiwon is standing in front of the Smart Fridge, mesmerized.
Rio is behind the DJ booth, pressing every button at once.
Yuri is in the lounge, arranging throw pillows for a photo.
Eden (The Mentor) is nowhere to be seen. I should check the balcony.
The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_night1_kang.text:
You walk over to the billiards area. Kang is leaning over the green felt, focused. Click. The cue ball strikes, sinking a striped ball into the corner pocket.

She doesn't look up, but she chalks her cue. "Penthouse," she says suddenly. "Excuse me?" "That's what I'm buying when I win," she straightens up, looking at you. "Top floor in Gangnam. Floor-to-ceiling windows. What about you? What's the first thing you buy?"

choices.text: A house for my parents. They've sacrificed enough.
A private island. I want to be as far away from people as possible.
I'm not thinking about the money. I just want the title.

villa_night1_kang1.text: Kang nods, a rare look of softness in her eyes. "Respect. That's... yeah. That's the real goal."

villa_night1_kang2.text: Kang chuckles, lining up the next shot. "I get that. Invite me when you get sick of the silence."

villa_night1_kang3.text: Kang pauses, studying you. "You can't live in a title. Be practical."

villa_night1_after_kang.text: She nods, a rare look of softness in her eyes. "Respect. That's... yeah. That's the real goal."

choices.text: Jiwon is standing in front of the Smart Fridge, mesmerized.
Rio is behind the DJ booth, pressing every button at once.
Yuri is in the lounge, arranging throw pillows for a photo.
Eden (The Mentor) is nowhere to be seen. I should check the balcony.
The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_night1_jiwon.text: Jiwon is waving her hand in front of the refrigerator door. The screen lights up, showing the weather and a recipe for avocado toast. "It talks," she whispers, eyes wide. "Did you hear that? It greeted me." She presses a button and ice cubes clatter into a glass. "Unlimited ice. It's magic."

choices.text: It's just a fridge, Jiwon.
Ask it if it knows who is going to win the show.
Grab me a soda while you're worshipping the machine.

villa_night1_after_jiwon.text: "It's not a fridge. It's a robot butler that feeds us."

choices.text: Rio is behind the DJ booth, pressing every button at once.
Yuri is in the lounge, arranging throw pillows for a photo.
Eden (The Mentor) is nowhere to be seen. I should check the balcony.
The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_night1_jiwon2.text:
Jiwon giggles and taps the screen again. "Hey, Fridge, am I going to debut?" The fridge beeps and flashes a random animation.

"It said yes! I think," she whispers, eyes sparkling.

villa_night1_jiwon3.text:
Jiwon presses another button and ice cubes clatter into a glass. She tosses you a can from the fridge.

"Sparkling water. Fancy," she says, trying to look casual but clearly delighted.

villa_night1_rio.text:
"Check 1, 2! Check!" Rio's voice booms through the expensive surround sound system. She is wearing huge headphones and messing with the mixer.

"This bass goes so low it vibrates my soul," she grins at you. "I think I just accidentally ordered pizza with this button. Or launched a missile. Either way, cool."

choices.text: Play something wild. Let's work on my twerk.
Careful, you'll blow the speakers.
Can you remix the doorbell sound?

villa_night1_after_rio.text: The music keeps pulsing in your chest long after you step away from the DJ booth. The Villa is still buzzing with energy, and the night isn't over yet.

choices.text: Yuri is in the lounge, arranging throw pillows for a photo.
Eden (The Mentor) is nowhere to be seen. I should check the balcony.
The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_night1_rio1.text: Rio drops a heavy beat. You do a quick attempt to wiggle. "Yesss! That's the energy!" RIO SHOUTS over the music, hyping you up like the center of the club.

villa_night1_rio2.text: You raise an eyebrow. "Careful, you'll blow the speakers." Rio rolls her eyes dramatically. "These speakers can handle armageddon. Live a little!" she laughs, turning the volume down by exactly one notch.

villa_night1_rio3.text: "Can you remix the doorbell sound?" you ask. Rio stares at you for a beat, then grins. "Challenge accepted. Let's make it a trap beat." Her fingers fly over the mixer as a ridiculous, menacing doorbell loop fills the room.

villa_night1_yuri.text:
Yuri isn't taking a selfie. She is staging the room. "If we move this lamp here... and throw this blanket casually there..." she steps back. "It looks like a catalogue. Come sit here. We need to look like we're candidly relaxing."

She laughs. "I've never been this relaxed in my life. This sofa is swallowing me."

choices.text: I'm diving in.
Is this velvet? I feel expensive just sitting here.
You're obsessed.

villa_night1_after_yuri.text: You flop onto the cushions. Yuri snaps a picture instantly. "Perfect. Natural chaos."

choices.text: Eden (The Mentor) is nowhere to be seen. I should check the balcony.
The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_night1_yuri1.text: You flop onto the cushions. Yuri snaps a picture instantly. "Perfect. Natural chaos."

villa_night1_yuri2.text: "Is this velvet?" you ask, sinking deeper into the sofa. "I feel expensive just sitting here." Yuri laughs, eyes lighting up. "Right? I feel like I should be holding a poodle."

villa_night1_yuri3.text: "You're obsessed," you tease. Yuri just smiles, adjusting a pillow by a few millimeters. "I'm thankful. There's a difference."

villa_night1_eden.text:
The glass door slides shut with a heavy click, finally cutting off the thumping bass from the living room. You step out onto the massive balcony, the cool night air hitting your skin.

You lean against the cold glass, letting out a long breath. The vibe inside is chaotic, too much noise. You just needed a moment of silence before your head exploded. But you aren't alone.

Eden is leaning against the far railing, a silhouette against the city lights. The red edge of his cigarette burns bright in the darkness. He doesn't turn immediately, letting the silence stretch, before finally glancing over his shoulder.

"Hakseng," he greets, his voice smooth, carrying a hint of smoke. "Good to have you here with us. I've always liked your Je Ne Sais Quoi and those... lines. You don't just move, you command the space." He turns fully toward you, ash falling from his cigarette. "Are you ready for the gauntlet?"

choices.text: I'm not sure yet, but I'll try my best.
Are you Halbae (old man)?
I'm always ready.
Depends on who will be cheering for me.
Stare at the stars next to Eden.

villa_night1_eden1.text:
Eden takes a deep, slow inhale. He purses his lips, blowing a perfect ring of smoke that drifts into the night air before dissolving. He stares up at the empty, starless sky.

"Aren't we all..." he murmurs, his voice dripping with a quiet exhaustion.

villa_night1_eden2.text:
Eden chokes. The smoke catches in his throat, and he coughs, startled out of his pose. He tilts his head, eyes widening, and then bursts into a genuine, sharp laugh.

"Well, aren't you a peach?" he wipes a tear from his eye, a crooked grin staying on his face. "It's fun. Maybe I'll keep you around."

villa_night1_eden3.text:
Eden stares at you, his expression flattening instantly. He drops the cigarette to the expensive tiles, crushes it under his heel and quietly turns away.

He walks past you back inside, as if you aren't even worth a response.

villa_night1_eden4.text:
Eden stops. The idol mask slips for a second, and he looks at you, really looks at you with a strange intensity.

"Friends come and go," he says quietly, counting on his fingers. "But the music you write, produce, and set out to the world? That is forever. Don't you forget that, ${name}."

villa_night1_eden_stars.text:
You join him at the railing, looking up.

"They're pretty, aren't they?" Eden asks softly. "But it's a lie. You're seeing light from three thousand years ago. They're already gone."

You immediately think about Jun. (Salt in the wound much?!)

Before you can answer, he pushes off the railing and walks away, leaving you alone in the dark without a goodbye.

choices.text: The excitement is draining me. I need to end the night.

villa_hallway_night.text: The lights in the main hall dim automatically. The day is finally ending. You head towards your room, but you stop. The hallway is not empty.

choices.text: I hear shouting and weird noises coming from the Media Room.
Heol. This hallway is giving me major first victim vibes. It is a total trap. (But let's be real, you are not here to be a survivor. You are here for the Plot. Go on, make a bad decision.)
Just go to my room. I am exhausted.

haneul_drums_scene.text: The music room is a chaos of sound tonight. Haneul is at the drum kit, lost in a trance of violence and rhythm. They haven't noticed you yet. Sweat drips down their neck as the sticks blur in their hands. You stand by the door, unseen, feeling like an intruder—or a perv. Either way, you can't look away.

minjae_garden_scene.text:
You slip out toward the west garden. In the shadows, someone is trying to climb the fence. A leg swings over, hesitates, and then—THUD. The figure lands in the mud with zero grace.

A neon pink plastic flamingo takes the hit like a champ. "F****** hell," the figure groans. You recognize the voice. Han Minjae. Heartthrob. Currently losing a fight to garden decor.

He spots you and straightens instantly, trying to recover his dignity. "You saw nothing," he says. "I was... testing the durability of the decor."

villa_media_room_coco.text:
You push open the door to the media lounge. On the massive TV, someone is screaming at someone else in a British accent. Coco is curled on the sofa, eyes glued to the screen.

"Geordie Shore," she says without looking away. "The British one. Don't judge. It's anthropological research."

You blink. "You're watching Geordie Shore?"

"I'm studying how humans behave when you remove all consequences and add alcohol." She finally glances at you, then back at the chaos. "It's genius. Also, I may have hacked the VPN." She gestures to the spot next to her. "Sit. I managed to hack the VPN. We have about 20 minutes before the firewall resets."

choices.text: Whatever you say. Please tell me that's Season 12?
Are you okay? You look like you're trying to escape.
You know the staff can track the viewing history, right?

coco_tv1.text:
You decide not to push the whole LA versus UK thing. Everyone has secrets.

Coco's eyes light up, relieved you dropped it. "A person of culture! Yes! It's the episode where they fight over a kebab."

You sit down. For the next ten minutes, you aren't trainees fighting for your lives. You're just two kids laughing at people making bad decisions. "God, look at them," Coco sighs happily. "They are so messy. It's beautiful. No choreography, just chaos."

coco_tv2.text:
"Are you okay?" you ask softly. "You look like you're trying to escape."

Coco pauses. The smile on her face flickers. "Escape? Maybe," she admits.

She looks at the screen, where everyone is hugging in a club in Newcastle. "It's just... everyone here speaks so politely. Even when they hate you, they use formal language. I just needed to hear someone scream 'Wot the f***' without filtering it. It sounds like..." She cuts herself off before saying 'home'. "...like freedom."

She nudges the popcorn bowl toward you. "Sit. Let's be messy spectators together."

coco_tv3.text:
"You know the staff can track the viewing history, right?" you say.

Coco rolls her eyes, regaining her composure. "Let them watch. Maybe you guys will learn how to have fun." But she still glances nervously at the door, the mood dipping for a second.

She tightens the blanket around herself. "Relax. It's just contraband culture. If the PD sees this, he'll probably exorcise the TV." She pats the cushion next to her. "Sit. If we go down, we go down laughing."

the_empty_bunk.text:
You don't bring up LA versus UK. Coco looks relieved. "There's an episode where they fight over a kebab," she says. "We're getting there."

You watch people on screen make bad decisions. Coco sighs. "Beautiful. No choreography, just chaos."

She produces a bowl. "Microwave popcorn. The cheap stuff." The smell hits you first—artificial butter, like nitrous in a leaking tank. A dangerous drift. The feeling of losing control.

You both reach for a handful at the same time. Your hands brush.

You freeze. It's like hitting a wall at a hundred miles an hour. The 70 inch screen blurs. All you can think is: *Calories. Fat. Butter. Grease.* You pull your hand back.

Coco is staring at you. Your face must be pale, wide eyed, breathing shallow. You look like you've seen a ghost. "Did you chip a tooth or something?"

"I can't," you stammer. "I have to go." You're backing away before you can stop yourself.

You run to the bathroom. You wash your hands. You wash your face. You stand there until your heart stops racing, then you go back to your room.

villa_room_night1.text:
You're in your room. Rio and Coco are on their beds, scrolling through NAVER. You're in an expensive, soft bed—better than the basement dorms. It still feels wrong.

You check the bottom bunk out of habit. Empty. Perfectly made. No Jun.

*We're going to make it, right?* Her voice in your head. She's probably at her parents' house right now. Eating ramen. Or crying. Survivor's guilt sits on your chest like a brick.

"You thinking about Jun?" Rio asks.

"Yeah."

"She's tough," Coco says, not looking up from her phone. "She'll be okay. Probably watching us on TV right now, judging my outfit."

You curl under the blanket. You made it to the Villa. But the climb is lonely.
villa_room_night1.nextButtonText: Day 2 dun dun dun - the Caste System

day2_caste_system.text:
6:00 AM. The wake-up call is not a suggestion. It's military. If you are one minute late, you are dead.

Ten minutes later, you're in the living room with the other trainees. Noa walks in. Perfect. Sharp white suit. She points to a massive digital scoreboard on the wall.

"The footage from the Intro Challenge is out," she says. "The Netizens have voted." Your name sits in dull grey in the bottom half of the list. **RANK: Hidden Gem. Follower Count: 9000 (Low Visibility).** Ouch. You have a long way to go. Sucks to be you right now.

Noa announces a pool party to celebrate the launch. "But a party needs guests... and it needs staff." Staff bring in two boxes: one with gold velvet wristbands, one with cheap plastic ones. "Top Tier—you're Masters. You enjoy the pool, the open bar, the massage chairs. Survival Tier—you're Servants. You pour the drinks. You fan the Masters. You cut their fruit."

The room freezes. *Does labor law exist in this universe?* you wonder. Probably not.

"This is your lesson for today," Noa says coldly. "Hierarchy. If you don't like serving... work harder."

"CUT!" The director's voice. The moment the camera stops rolling, the Ice Queen melts. Noa slumps her shoulders instantly. She looks at the terrified trainees, puffing out her cheeks and crossing her eyes in a goofy, derp face. She wiggles her fingers mockingly at her own script. "So dramatic, right? 'Work harder!'" She whispers to you with a wink. "I hate saying that stuff."

"We're rolling in 3... 2... 1..."

SNAP. Noa straightens her back. The terrifying smile returns instantly. "Choose your bands. Now."

choices.text: I take the Plastic band and salute mockingly. 'At your service, Your Highness.'
I glare at the gold team. I will remember every face that orders me around.

day2_hierarchy_plastic.text:
You snap the cheap plastic onto your wrist. It scratches your skin. "Okay," you say loud enough for the cameras. "I make a terrible Mojito, but I'm great at spilling things accidentally."

A few trainees laugh. You reclaimed the power by making it a joke. Even Noa's lip twitches—she definitely liked that one.

day2_free_time.text:
The cameras retreat for the afternoon. The Villa is open to you, but the wristbands create an invisible wall. The Gold Rank trainees are lounging on the leather sofas. The Plastic Rank trainees are huddled in the kitchen.

You have some free time. What do you do?

choices.text: I go to the Home Gym. (Interaction: Kang)
I look for a quiet place in the Garden. (Interaction: Jiwon & Yuri)
I check out the Game Room. (Interaction: Rio)

day2_gym_kang.text: You walk into the state-of-the-art gym. It smells of iron and sweat. Kang is there, of course. She is doing pull-ups with a weight belt strapped to her waist. She is wearing a Gold band. "105... 106..." she grunts, muscles straining. She hasn't seen you yet.

choices.text: I stay silent and start my own workout. Let the weights speak.
"I saw you reading a romance novel in the locker room. 'The Duchess's Secret Love'?"

day2_gym_kang_after.text: You grab a set of dumbbells. Kang pauses at the top of the bar, glancing at you in the mirror. She appreciates the silence. She drops down, wiping sweat with a towel. "Good form," she grunts. She sits on the bench press, unbuckling the heavy belt. "I turned 18 last week," she says suddenly. The voice is flat. "In the real world, I'm barely an adult. In this building? I'm a fossil. The PDs want fresh meat. If I don't debut in this group... I'm done."

choices.text: "Then stop training like a robot. Show them you have something the kids don't: Experience."
"You're the best dancer here. Don't let the numbers scare you."

day2_dinner_day3.text:
Kang looks up. "Maybe you're right. I need to stop being perfect and start being dangerous." She stands up, looking focused. The sun sets over the Villa. Dinner is served. The masters are eating ribeye steak with truffle butter, while the servants are looking at bowls of white rice and spicy kimchi.

You look at your plate. It's a standard portion, but your brain doesn't see 'food'—it sees data points. Rice: *204 calories (assuming 150g)*. Kimchi: *23 calories*. Total for this sitting: *227*.

You eat precisely three-quarters of the rice, leaving a neat little mound on the left side of the bowl. It's not that you aren't hungry—the hunger is a dull roar in the back of your mind—but the math has to be right. *227* is a 'safe' number for a Thursday.

You chew slowly, counting the jaw movements. *32 times per bite*. Optimal digestion. Minimum bloat. It's just a routine. No big deal.

day2_gameroom_rio.text:
Rio is sprawled on a beanbag chair, staring intensely at the TV. On the screen, a 2D anime character is blushing.

"Take the cookies, Kenji," Rio whispers aggressively. "Just take them, I spent 372 coins on flour!"

choices.text: "Are you... playing a dating simulator?"
"I think you need to choose the 'Shy' dialogue option."

day2_gameroom_rio_after.text:
On screen, Kenji accepts the cookies. **AFFINITY +50!**

Rio drops the controller. A single, dramatic tear rolls down their cheek.

"He took them," Rio chokes out. "Beautiful. I'm not crying. You're crying. Look at that pixelated smile."

Then, the screen fades to black for a second. In the reflection, you see Rio's face. The smile is gone. When the music starts again, the smile snaps back onto their face instantly. "Okay! Beach date. Let's go."

choices.text: Observe the reflection and sit quietly with Rio.
"You are obsessed. I'm going to get a snack."

day3_morning_plan.text:
Ahh, morning. The sun is up, the birds are singing, and there is a high-definition camera lens exactly three inches away from your drooling face.

You still haven't quite gotten used to the fact that the camera staff are basically your permanent tail. You are, however, deeply moved by the fact that the staff already knows your coffee order by heart. Small wins.

Still, no rest for the wicked.

As you brush your hair and comb your teeth—apparently reality TV brain fog is finally affecting your basic motor skills—you begin to plan the rest of your stat-enhancing day. I mean, your totally relaxing "free day off", of course.

You catch a glimpse of Yuri in the hallway, looking like a deer in headlights as a boom mic hovers just a bit too close to her head. Even the seasoned pros are looking a bit twitchy today.

Time to make the most of the silence. What is the plan?

choices.text: I lock myself in the karaoke room and sing until the staff starts checking their earplugs.
I hit the dance studio and sweat until the floor becomes a genuine safety hazard.
I spend the morning practicing my "spontaneous" idol expressions in the mirror.
I find a blind spot and sleep like a person who actually values their sanity.

day3_morning_sing.text: You lock yourself in the karaoke room and start running scales. You sing until your throat feels like glass, then drink some lukewarm water and do it again. It's not glamorous, but your pitch is finally stable.

day3_morning_dance.text: You hit the dance studio and sweat until the floor becomes a genuine safety hazard. You practice the same isolation move 500 times, then 500 more. Pain is just a great narrative arc for the viewers at home, right? At least you look sharp.

day3_morning_mirror.text: You spend hours practicing "spontaneous" idol expressions in the mirror. It feels ridiculous with a camera operator standing right behind you, but in this industry, your face is your primary weapon.

day3_morning_sleep.text: You find a sunbed in a tiny corner of the garden, blessedly outside the main camera angles. You actually sleep. Real sleep. You wake up feeling like a human being instead of a caffeinated ghost.

day3_evening_day4.text:
You run scales until your throat feels like glass, then you drink some lukewarm water and do it again. It's not glamorous, but your pitch is finally stable. The sun sets on Day 3. The Villa is a pressure cooker. Everyone is preparing for tomorrow. The Pool Party.

You pull a protein bar from your bag. You've memorized the label already, but you check it again. *212 calories. 18g protein. 7g sugar.*

You don't throw it away. You need the energy for the pool tomorrow, but you can't just eat it. That would be chaotic. Using a small plastic knife, you cut the bar into exactly eight equal squares. Each square is *26.5 calories*.

You eat four of them. *106 calories exactly*. You feel a strange sense of peace as you put the other four back in the wrapper. Everything is under control. The math adds up. You fall asleep calculating your intake and training sessions per muscle group for the week.

day4_pool_party_prep.text:
08:00 AM. Jun's voice. A ringing sound. The intercom crackles. "Good morning, Trainees. The Styling Room is now open. Select your outfit. The party begins at 10:00 AM sharp."

You drag yourself out of bed. Your hair is a mess. Two hours to become a god.

Noa is waiting by the wardrobe racks. White linen suit. She looks perpetually flawless. "Servants," she says. "You wear the staff uniform. White polo shirts and beige shorts—clean, professional, invisible."

You pick up the uniform. Rio is holding the shorts with two fingers. "I look like a slice of toast with jam."

Noa doesn't smile. "Get changed. Cameras are live in 30 minutes."

You head to the stalls. The hierarchy is set. The sun is out. Let's see who sinks and who swims.

day4_pool_party.text:
The sun is a blinding white spotlight. The air smells of chlorine, SPF 50, and the heavy scent of classism. The courtyard is the epitome of luxury—white leather sunbeds by turquoise water. Waiters glide toward the Gold Bands with drinks. The Plastic Bands get sympathy nods.

A figure appears on the second-floor balcony. The PD. He's wearing a floral silk robe and holding a feathered fan and an Iced Americano. He looks like a Roman Emperor staring down at a pit of gladiators.

"Welcome, my little icons!" His voice booms. "Luxurious Minimalism. We spent the budget on 8K lenses to see your pores. So, no chairs for the Servants. My life is in St. Tropez." He snaps his fingers. "Masters—take your seats. Servants—extras don't sit. They SWEAT."

The Servants stand in the sun in scratchy white polo shirts. A screech cuts through the music. The PD points his fan at a trainee trying to hide bloodshot eyes behind dark shades. "You! With the sunglasses! OFF! NOW!" He leans precariously over the railing. "I want to see despair in your eyes in 8K resolution! Sunglasses are for Masters only! You want to be a burger flipper? Show me emotion!" The trainee removes the glasses, blinking. The PD sips his coffee.

Benji the scrawny PA tries to slip an unbranded water bottle to a trainee who's about to faint. The PD spots it. He grabs a megaphone. "BENJI! Is that... an UNBRANDED water bottle in my frame?!" Benji freezes. "Sir, they were literally vibrating from thirst."

"I don't care if they are spontaneously combusting!" The PD roars. "If I see another logoless bottle, I am selling your kidney on eBay! Do you know how much shipping costs from Seoul to LA? Focus, for God's sake! This is art, not a charity!" He tosses the megaphone aside and fans himself dramatically. "Action in 5 minutes! Don't be boring! The drones are hungry!"

Drones buzz overhead like angry, high-tech hornets. The heat is rising. The fakeness of this "party" is so thick you could cut it with a knife.

How do you handle the start of the madness?

choices.text: I dive in. If the world is a snake pit, I'll be the one with the sharpest fangs.

I disconnect. I sit in the center of the storm and let the world burn around me. (Zen Guru Path: Be warned, this option will skip a significant gameplay but will give a unique angle instead + infinite chill)

day4_art_of_forced_fun.text: You clench your fists. If they want a show, I'll give them a blockbuster. You step into the crowd, ready to fight for every second of airtime.

day4_visual_battle_royale.text:
The PD leans over the marble railing, fanning himself. He looks bored. "Youthful Radiance, people. I'm not seeing it. Give me something."

The trainees scramble. Yuri—quiet, elegant, Gold Band on her wrist—walks to the edge of the pool. There's a mischievous glint in her eyes. "It's just water." She jumps. SPLASH. Messy, happy. The Masters cheer. Rio grabs a water gun. The Villa actually feels like a party.

You join the water gun fight, a temporary alliance with Rio. For a moment you forget you're in a fight for your life. Jiwon is watching Yuri laugh. She forgets the Plastic Band on her wrist. She forgets the rules. "Wait for me!" She jumps in. SPLASH. A cannonball, right next to Yuri. They surface. High-five. Hair plastered to their faces. Jiwon is laughing. "The water is amazing!"

The laughter dies. Rio freezes. The other Masters stop splashing. Everyone looks at the balcony.

The PD slowly lowers his Iced Americano. "Yuri, darling. Exquisite entry. Very joie de vivre." His gaze shifts. "Jiwon... sweetie." He sighs, sounding deeply exhausted. "I know the education system failed many of you, but I assumed you could read."

Jiwon's smile vanishes. She looks at Yuri. At the water. She's the only Servant in the pool. "I... Sir, I..."

"The sign says Masters Only." The PD's voice drips with condescension. "Yuri is a Master. You are contaminating my VIP aesthetic with your... peasant enthusiasm. It's giving anarchy, and not the chic kind. Get out. Before I deduct the cost of the pool cleaning from your non-existent paycheck." He waves his fan dismissively.

Jiwon scrambles toward the ladder, face burning red. Yuri reaches for her. Jiwon pulls away, mortified, apologizing, mumbling. To climb out faster she yanks off the heavy, soaking-wet hoodie she'd been wearing over her uniform and tosses it onto the tiles. It's a smooth motion. "It's choking me," she whispers.

The PD stops fanning himself. He stares. Jiwon's revelation—the damp tank top clinging to an athletically defined body. Sculpted shoulders. A core of steel. The PD takes off his sunglasses. Squints. "Is that... an 8-pack?"

He throws his coffee over his shoulder. "SMASH. FORGET THE RULES! Camera 2—you're filming the water! ZOOM IN ON THE TORSO!"

Jiwon crosses her arms, shrinks back. "Should I... get out?"

"Don't you dare hide." The PD's voice is honey-sweet. "We missed the reveal. Too fast. Messy." He points a manicured finger. "Benji—Mist her! We are doing a reshoot. Get back in the water. Put the hoodie back on." "But it's soaking—" "I don't care if it's made of lead! Put it on! And this time... take it off slowly. Give me Megan Fox opening the car hood vibes. Give me Pride and Prejudice Colin Firth. Give me Captain America summons Thor's hammer. Annnnnd action!"

Jiwon sighs. The light leaves her eyes. The fun is over. She does it again—staring dead-eyed into the lens, turning a moment of joy into a thirst trap.

The PD screams. "YES! That is the thumbnail! I am a god! Benji, buy yourself a cookie, you're not fired today!" His boredom returns instantly. "Enough frolicking. SWIMMING RACE! Masters vs Servants! If a Servant wins, I'll let you stand in the shade for five minutes. GO!"

day4_swimming_race.text:
The whistle blows. The pool explodes into chaos.

Let's be honest: putting 30 aspiring idols in one infinity pool isn't a swimming race. It's a mosh pit. It is tighter than a rookie group's van. It is more chaotic than a music show ending stage where everyone is fighting for the 'Center' position. There is no water left. Only limbs, waterproof mascara, and desperate ambition.

The water is freezing, the elbows are sharp, and the desire to drown your competitors is rising.

choices.text: I fight for the Center position. Get out of my way.
I swim for the Fancam. Slow, pretty, and useless.

day4_swimming_race1.text:
You bury your head and kick. You channel "Main Dancer" energy and plow through the water. You don't care who you hit; you're fighting for screen time. You emerge from the crush of bodies, gasping for air, hair plastered to your face, looking like you just survived a zombie apocalypse.

Up on the balcony, the PD yawns into the microphone. "You made it. You swim with the grace of a malfunctioning blender. Fast, but aesthetically offensive."

day4_swimming_race2.text:
You realize there is no point in racing in this human soup. So you slow down. You find a pocket of space, making sure your wet hair looks styled and not drowned rat. You give the drone a slow, smoldering look.

You finish dead last—but you emerge from the water like a slow-motion shampoo commercial.

The PD claps lazily. "Finally! Someone who understands that cardio is the enemy of a good close-up. Ten points for holding the visual."

day4_friction_point.text:
You bury your head and kick. You channel 'Main Dancer' energy. When you finally surface, the PD is shouting something at the swimmers—then he gets distracted by his phone. You catch a glimpse through the glass railing: he's aggressively zooming in on Eden's hands over the mixing console. He types furiously. Posts something. The username flashes before he swipes away: @Eden_Is_My_Garden_99.

Coco climbs out of the pool. She walks right up to him. The PD startles. "I was—checking stocks! High-stakes business stocks!" Coco doesn't blink. "The vibe is tired. Have you heard what they're playing in LA?" The PD puffs up. "This is my curated Tropical House playlist." Coco shrugs. "It's forced swimming rubbish." The PD's fan stills. "That's a very... colonial thing to say."

Coco freezes. The color drains from her face for a microsecond. She realizes the slip-up immediately. "Trash!" Coco corrects loudly, forcing an exaggerated American accent. "I meant trash! Rubbish... is just what my... uh... British nanny used to say. Yeah. Nanny. She was awful. A total hag." Coco laughs nervously. "Anyway. LA vibes. You know?"

You shrug, your mind drifting with the gentle current of the pool. A British nanny? It sounds exactly like the kind of pretentious, over-the-top thing a rich kid from LA would have. The PD seems to buy it too—or maybe he's just too busy fumbling with his phone to care about linguistics.

day4_chicken_fight.text:
Sang-ho claps his hands. "Listen up! I want violence. I want betrayal. I want high stakes drama." He grins. "Chicken Fight. Horse and Rider. Last pair dry(ish) gets the VIP bed. I want to see chemistry—or lack thereof—between Masters, Servants."

The pool turns into a frenzy. Rio and Coco have already paired up. Rio is the horse—solid, muscular, grinning like a feral wolf. Coco is perched on her shoulders, looking down at the rest of us like royalty. She winks at the camera. "Come and get it, losers."

You need a partner. And you need one fast. You look at the water level. Most trainees are standing chest-deep. You are neck-deep, bobbing like a cork. Whoever gets on your shoulders is going to have a very wet, very panic-inducing ride. The PD notices and cackles. "Can someone get ${name} a snorkel? We don't have insurance for drowning!"

choices.text: Haneul. (Platonic) "We stick together, buddy."
Haneul. (Romantic) "I want you close."
Noa. The Host. (Power couple) "Make me."

day4_chicken_fight_haneul.text:
You wade toward Haneul. Haneul gasps. "${name}! Rio is huge. We're going to get crushed." You offer to be the horse. "Hop on." Haneul swallows hard. "But if I scream, just ignore it." He climbs onto your shoulders and screeches. "Why is the water touching my chin?! ${name}, I'm not a submarine!" "Hush," you strain under the weight, blowing bubbles. "It's stealth mode." Then: "FIGHT!"

Rio charges like a bulldozer. Coco laughs maniacally on top. Your partner tightens their grip. You're barely keeping your nose above the surface. A wave hits Haneul. He sputters. "Blargh! I'm drinking the pool! ${name}, periscope up! Periscope up!" Rio is aiming for your chest, but you're low in the water. The collision happens in slow motion. Rio slams into the water like a cannonball, creating a wave that hits you in the face. Coco leans forward, reaching out to shove your partner.

choices.text: Use my Dance balance (and low center of gravity). (Dance > 50)
Use Charisma. Psychological Warfare. (Charisma > 55)
Rely on Synchronization. (Relationship Check)

day4_chicken_fight_noa.text:
You wade toward the Host herself. "Noa," you say, offering your hand. "Let's save the ratings."

She stares at your hand for a long beat, eyes narrowed. For a second you wonder if you've overplayed it. Then, with a tiny huff, she kicks off her heels and takes your hand.

"Fine," she mutters. "But if you drop me, I will kill you."

The Villa goes silent as the Host steps into the pool. She climbs onto your shoulders, legs wrapping around your neck. The water rises dangerously close to her waist.

"Why is this so deep?" she hisses into your ear. "Are you just... compact?"

"Travel-sized," you grunt, standing on your tiptoes. "Aerodynamic."

Somewhere above, the PD screams into his megaphone. "THE HOST IS IN THE POOL?! I LOVE IT! ZOOM IN ON THE TENSION!"

Rio charges like a bulldozer. Coco cackles on top. Your partner tightens her grip. The collision happens in slow motion; a wall of water and eyeliner.

day4_calm_before_storm.text:
You try to pivot, but the water resistance is too strong. Rio simply walks *over* you. "Wait, where did they go?" Rio asks, looking around. You are currently underwater, being crushed by the defeat. The splash clears. The winner is decided.

You surface, coughing up chlorine. Coco is high-fiving Rio. "Nice try, babe," Coco winks. "But the bed is mine." Haneul surfaces slowly, maintaining poise. He wipes his eyes and offers a tired nod. "A disappointing outcome. My balance was... insufficient." The PD sighs. "Coco and Rio take the win! Everyone else... enjoy your mediocrity."

day4_handle_the_brat.text:
The water settles. The adrenaline fades. Losers are toweling off, shivering. Winners are gloating. You climb out of the pool, sore. The camera is still rolling.

A loud SPLASH. Haneul is trying to climb onto a giant inflatable pink flamingo. He fails. The flamingo flips. He goes face-down in the water, legs flailing. The PD screams through the megaphone: "YES! THE FLAMINGO STRIKES AGAIN!" Haneul resurfaces, looking utterly betrayed by the bird.

The DJ switches the track. From aggressive EDM to a slow, heavy, hypnotic R&B beat. Noa is standing on the sidelines, pristine in a silk robe, looking down at the wet trainees. She starts to walk toward the villa—then stops. The bass kicks in. She closes her eyes. Her body moves. Sharp isolation of the ribcage. A fluid, liquid roll of the hips. She drops her robe onto a chair and walks to the center of the empty dance floor. She's the only dry person there. Electric. Magnetic. She's not a celebrity right now. She's just an artist.

BZZZZZ. A camera drone drops from the sky, buzzing inches from her face. The money shot. Noa's trance breaks. Her eyes snap open and narrow into dangerous slits. With the reflexes of a viper, she grabs a silver champagne bucket from a nearby table and swings it. CLANG. She catches the drone. Slams the bucket down. Traps it. The music stops. The PD gasps. Noa looks into the camera. She's furious. But her voice is steady. "Deduct it from my paycheck." She storms toward the villa.

The PD walks over to you. He looks bored. "Okay, the water fight was cute. But I need meme-worthy content." He points his fan at Coco. She's sixteen, a bored Princess sprawling on a sunbed, holding a glass of blue gin on national television. Pure, unadulterated apathy mixed with a 'try me' attitude. The PD barks. "Go. Friction. Now."

You walk over. Coco blocks your path with her leg. "Servant. Refill. Or get lost."

How do you handle the brat?

choices.text: Oi, Lucifer.
Hey, Honeypoo.
Sunbae... my Sunflower (Haebaragi).
Jagiya.

day4_magic_of_editing.text:
You kick the leg of the sunbed. Hard. The gin splashes onto her hand. "Wake up, Lucifer. You're not at home."

Coco's eyes snap open. She stares at the spilled alcohol. "You owe me a drink." Her voice is cold. "Do that again and I'll bite you."

Min-ho—the Red Shirt trainee—steps in, blocking your view. "Back off." His jaw is tight. "Can't you see Coco is tired? Stop bothering the visuals." Yuri was tuning a guitar nearby. She sets it down. "Min-ho, stop. They were just talking. Don't make a scene." Min-ho turns on her. "Oh, Jiwon's little shadow. Ghost Singer in a trainee's costume. Know your place." Coco stands up. "That's too far." Yuri drops the guitar and runs toward the gardens, shoulders shaking.

day4_behind_the_scenes_nighttime.text: "AND... CUT!" The PD's voice cuts through the chaos. He's squinting at a monitor, worried about the lighting. "We're wrapping for the day. Angels—listen up. Do not change your clothes. Do not remove your makeup. Continuity. We're shooting the Aftermath scene at 6:00 AM. I want you looking exactly like you do right now. Exhausted. Real. Got it?" He doesn't wait for an answer. The crew starts packing. Around you, exhausted teenagers smelling of chlorine stare at nothing. The magic of editing hasn't started yet. This is just the raw footage of survival.

day4_nighttime_who_to_find.text:
The Villa is quiet. Outside the glass walls, the lights of Seoul shimmer in the distance. Most trainees have collapsed in the common room, careful not to ruin their "Continuity Look" too much.

But two people are missing.

Yuri never came back after running to the gardens. (Well, she did sneak into the pool area to take her guitar, but that's it. I swear.)

Noa was seen heading up to the secluded VIP balcony with a bottle of something expensive.

You have a moment before you pass out. Who do you look for?

choices.text: Go find Yuri in the gardens.
Go find Noa on the VIP Balcony.

day4_nighttime_yuri.text:
The gardens are dark, curated. Tropical heat lamps create a misty, surreal atmosphere. You find Yuri sitting on a stone bench, hugging her knees to her chest. The gold velvet band of the Master rank sits on her wrist. A guitar is lying on the grass, forgotten.

"Go away..." she croaks. Her voice is thick with tears. "The cameras aren't here."

"That's why I'm here," you say softly, sitting on the other end of the bench.

Yuri shakes her head. "I'm a fraud. I'm just a ghost. Min-ho was right." She takes a shaky breath. "I recorded the high notes for Let's Kill This Dove. The idols lip-synced. I got paid. They got the fame. And now... now I'm here, pretending I'm a star. I tricked everyone. The PD is going to expose me."

The air is too heavy. You need to break the tension before she spirals completely.

How do you try to make her smile?

choices.text: Do a brutal impression of Min-ho's voice crack.
Tell her about your own embarrassing moment today.
Make a dark joke. Being a ghost is better than being a puppet.

day4_nighttime_yuri_after.text:
You stand up and puff out your chest. "YOU!" you shout in a cracked, whiny voice, pointing a finger. "How DARE you be talented! My hair gel is more important than your vocals!"

Yuri blinks, startled. Then a snort escapes her. "He doesn't sound like that," she protests weakly, but the corners of her mouth twitch. "He sounds worse," you correct, sitting back down.

The mood shifts. It's still sad, but the panic has subsided. Yuri takes a deep breath. "So... what do I do? How do I walk in there tomorrow?"

choices.text: "The voice IS the Star Quality. Everything else is just packaging."
"I feel the same way. Like I'm just acting the part until I get caught." [Acquire Trait: Imposter Syndrome]

day5_morning_after_yuri.text: "Without you, their songs are empty," you say firmly. "You aren't a fraud. You're the engine. Now it's time to drive the car." Yuri looks at you, touching the Gold band. A small glimmer of hope returns. "Thanks, ${name}," Yuri whispers, picking up the guitar. "I... I think I'll stay here a bit longer. Practice until I feel like I deserve this Gold."

day4_nighttime_noa_balcony.text:
[i]The Balcony[/i]

She's leaning over the railing, a crystal glass loosely held in her fingers. A heavy bottle of vintage red rests on the concrete floor beside her.

She takes a sip, swirling the dark liquid. She looks calm. Too calm for someone who just demolished a drone.

"2018 Vintage," she murmurs. "Supposed to have notes of oak and blackberry. I just taste the headache."

choices.text: "I saw a Red Flag and I thought: That looks like a fun challenge."
"I came to see the view. You're just blocking it."
"I just wanted to see if you were okay."
"I brought you a theoretical stress ball. You look like you're about to explode."

day4_noa_siblings_talk.text:
You stand together in the silence, watching the city lights below. "Do you have siblings?" Noa asks suddenly, swirling the wine in her glass like she's studying a storm. After you answer, she nods once.

"I have a brother," she says, like she's reading a neutral line off a résumé. "Older. Eight years. He has kids now. My nieces. He won't let me see them." She taps a fingernail against the glass. "He found God. Or rather, a version of God that doesn't like mini skirts or crop tops. He thinks what I do... this whole industry... is sinful."

She gestures at her outfit, at the Villa, at the distant cameras. "My dad used to say, 'Noa isn't bad, she's just loud.' But he's gone." For a heartbeat, the performance drops. The Host looks small, almost human. "So tell me, Agaya," she asks quietly. "Am I a sin? Or am I just loud?"

choices.text: "You aren't a sin, Noa. You're a force of nature."
"Honestly? You're kind of scary."
"Prove him wrong. Be so successful he can't look away."

day5_morning_after_noa.text:
Eventually, the wine runs low and the night air turns cold. Noa straightens her shoulders, the Host mask sliding neatly back into place. "Enough feelings for one evening," she mutters, but her voice is softer. "Don't make me regret this conversation."

You make your way back through the quiet Villa. The last thing you remember is the city lights fading outside the window as your eyes finally close.

When you wake, the sun is already rising over the water. Day 5 has begun.

day5_sumin_fall.text:
The sun rises over the Villa. 6:00 AM. "WAKE UP, ANGELS!" The speakers vibrate.

The PD is waiting by the pool. He's wearing a neon pink visor and holding an Iced Americano like it's a scepter. "Hello, Hello, Hello!" he calls out, his voice sing-song and loud. He scans the group of exhausted trainees and claps his hands dramatically. "Look at this lineup! Giving me Survivor: Fashion Week realness. I love the bags under the eyes, very chic, very I haven't slept since 2019." He waves his feathered fan toward the buffet table. "Fuel up, Queens and Kings! I need energy! If you faint, you better do it gracefully, or I'm editing it out. Choices!"

You walk toward the buffet. It's a spread of greasy bacon, sugary pancakes, and heavy cream. (Trait: Eating Disorder active) You look at the food. You don't see breakfast. You see an equation. Pancakes + Syrup = A number that ruins the week. Bacon = Variable X (Grease). It's not fear. It's just... math. If the math doesn't add up, the control slips. And if you lose control here, you lose everything. You grab a single slice of melon (30 calories, safe, verified data) and walk away.

As you turn around, you bump into Benji, the stressed PA. "Oh god, sorry, ${name}," he whispers. He leans in close, checking if the PD is watching. "Off the record," he murmurs. "Step it up this weekend." "Why?" "I saw the schedule. They're bringing in focus groups on Saturday. Civilians." He swallows hard. "They aren't looking for the Villain. TV needs villains. They're looking for the furniture." Benji eyes the cameras nervously. "The PD wants to eliminate the boring ones. If you don't make them feel something—love, hate, anything—you're gone." "BENJI!" The PD screams. "Why is my cup empty?! I don't pay you to gossip!" "Coming, sir!" Benji yelps and runs off.

The filming continues for hours. By 14:00, the heat is a physical weight, pressing down on the Villa. The air is thick with the smell of chlorine, SPF50, and despair. You're standing near the deep end of the pool, adjusting your cap. Suddenly, someone gasps. You look up.

Su-min is standing on the second-floor terrace railing above the pool. The quiet girl whose name the cameras always skip. "I can do it..." she mutters. "Just give me one shot. Even if it's a split screen." She's balancing on the very edge of the marble ledge, thirty feet above the water, trying to replicate that iconic, effortless high-dive Noa did in her last music video. She smiles for the drone—a haunting, porcelain-doll smile that doesn't reach her glazed eyes. "Su-min, get down from there!" Coco shouts. It's the first time Lucifer has sounded genuinely scared. Su-min's knees buckle. Her eyes roll back. Her body goes limp, tipping forward like a marionette whose strings were suddenly cut. The impact is a heavy, dead thud. A violent, flat impact. She doesn't resurface. A streak of red begins to surface in the water. The Villa erupts into pure, unfiltered chaos. Trainees are screaming. Rio is sprinting toward the edge, yelling for a medic.

choices.text: Dive in immediately. Screw the cameras.
Freeze. Analyze the situation.
Scream for help but stay in frame. Make her tragedy your stage.

day5_after_sumin_cpr.text:
You dive. You pull her out. You do CPR until your arms burn. She breathes. You've never felt relief like that.

The PD, Sang-ho, is on the balcony. He's not running. He's watching. He's *enjoying* it. "Don't cut!" he barks into his headset. "This is raw. This is Art." The medics finally take Su-min away, coughing up pink-tinged water. Sang-ho whispers into his headset: "Gorgeous. Episode cliffhanger. We're done for today." He dismisses the crew. "You're off the clock until Sunday morning. Go... process." The crew looks broken.

You remember Benji's whisper. The Focus Groups are tomorrow. Saturday. Sang-ho didn't mention them. He wants everyone relaxed. He wants to catch them off guard.

What will you do with this information?

choices.text: Tell the others. We all need to be ready.
Keep it to yourself. Let them fail.

day5_tell_others.text: "Wait, guys," you whisper, gathering the others. "Don't get too comfortable. Focus Groups are tomorrow. We need to stay sharp even if the cameras seem off." They look at you with surprised gratitude.

day6_pov_noa.text:
POV: NOA

23:00. The pool deck is finally quiet. The cameras are sleeping—at least, the ones the crew controls are. I've shed the glittering armor. I'm in an expensive, oversized streetwear hoodie, loose sweatpants and bare feet. I look like a runaway. My hair is disaster, tangled and salty. I walk to the edge of the pool and kick a plastic cup. My kingdom. This is my house. My water. My silence.

"You missed the action, honey!" I watch the moon on the water. I confront him about the pixels in real-time. "Your pacing was off. You let the girl stay blue for too long." Sang-ho approaches in designer loafers, a glass of cognac in one hand and his tablet in the other. The artificial blue glow of his tablet illuminates his face. That smile looks like it practiced being charming in a mirror. "Oh, Mother, please..." He rolls his eyes. "The audience lives for the blue tones. It makes them feel like they're seeing something raw. Something they aren't supposed to see. She was giving tragic mermaid and the servers were eating it up. But I'll check the pixels, darling. Since you're so concerned about the quality of my work." I snap back. "She's a teenager, Sang-ho. Not a prop. If you push them until they break, you don't have a show, you have a crime scene."

The air between us shifts. The flamboyant tilt of his head disappears. His spine straightens. That fabulous sparkle in his eyes vanishes, replaced by a cold, professional void. The sassy PD is gone. The man who survived 15 years in showbiz is back. "Don't get it twisted, Noa." His voice drops an octave. Flat and dangerously quiet. "You're at the top of every chart, the biggest star in this hemisphere. You could be doing anything right now. And yet, the board at Titan is still wondering why you decided to host a summer camp for nobodies in your own private sanctuary." He turns his head just enough to catch my profile, his eyes sharp in the digital light. "They're wondering if you're looking for a successor because you're losing your edge, or if you've actually gone soft. And trust me, honey, in this industry, soft is just another word for expired." I take a long sip of my chocolate milk from the crystal glass. The cold sweetness is a sharp contrast to the biting night air. "I was bored," I lie, making the lie sound like the most fascinating thing he's ever heard. "My hiatus was getting too quiet. I needed fresh energy to consume." The switch flips back. Sang-ho lets out a sharp, high-pitched giggle. The diva mask slides back into place as if it never left. He clicks his tongue, looking at me with that fake, admiring warmth. "Well, your energy just broke the servers. Su-min's collapse is the clip of the year. I'm going to make it look legendary." "Make it look like I planned it." I say it with a sharp edge to my smile. "Goodnight, Sang-ho." He retreats. His footsteps fade until the villa finally stops pretending to be a television set.

I'm alone. The silence here is different now. It isn't the hollow, ringing silence of the two years I spent staring at my mother's empty chair. It's... crowded. I look up at the massive structure. Damn, I think, the thought tasting like copper in my mouth. Am I really recreating Neverland like some kind of Peter Pan creep? I remember the day, age 10, when the noise stopped for the first time. The sound of a car pulling out of the driveway and never coming back. Then, years later, the sound of a hospital monitor turning into a flat, steady hum. I hope those kids won't hear that hum. From the looks of it I need to be more on my toes for that to happen. Na-ri isn't bad, she's just loud, my father used to say. Well, I've been loud enough to drown out the ghosts for years. But today, the silence won.

I look at the dark water. This constant state of high alert, the skin-tight performance I wear for the world... it all feels like a lead weight. My brain is too sharp for its own good, dissecting every emotion before I can even feel it. "Enough." I whisper to the moon. I don't dive. I don't perform. I turn my back to the pool, close my eyes, and let go. I just fall backward. A trust fall into the nothingness. **SPLASH.** I sink until the marble floor presses against my spine. The water is loud in my ears, a heavy pressure that feels like those long, silent drives in the back of the embassy cars. (Twenty years.) I kick the marble bottom, the vibration traveling up my legs. (They were together for twenty years. They had nine whole years before I was even a "mistake" in Malibu. My brother was eight when I was born. He got eighteen years of them being "real." I only got ten. Then the strings snapped.) I notice a cracked tile near the drain. I pick at it with my thumb, obsessing over the sharp edge. It's broken. I want to fix it. I want to glue it back so no one cuts their feet, because I couldn't fix the house in Seoul when the walls started bleeding silence. Then, there's you. ${name}. I let my hair drift like seaweed, my eyes fixed on the lights above. **Pulse.** **Reckless.** A hero act. You've got too much heart for a place where everyone is a predator. You're going to get hurt, and I don't want to be the one standing near you when the fallout hits. The morning is coming. I hope you're ready for the blood to turn into a storyline. I break the surface, gasping for the humid air. The chaos is still there, but I'm already gone. I climb out, my movements sharp and jagged against the biting midnight air. The water on my skin feels like needles as the wind hits me. I grab my thick white robe, wrapping it tight around myself like a shroud, pulling the hood low until my face is nothing but a shadow. I walk toward the Villa, my wet footsteps leaving a cold, shimmering trail of "broken" all the way to the door.

day6_red_morning.text:
The sky outside the floor-to-ceiling glass is bruised, bleeding red. Armageddon swallowing the Villa. Salt-crusted footprints mark the marble. Su-min is in a hospital somewhere.

Noa is leaning against the mahogany island. Hoodie. Bare feet. She still has that Icon glow even though she looks dead. Eden walks in carrying black coffees. He tries to lean coolly against the grand piano. He misses slightly and coughs to cover it. "Su-min is 'exhausted' according to the news." Eden addresses the room. "Sang-ho is already spinning it. Exploiting her." Rio is sitting, rubbing her chest. Her breathing is loud and shallow. She keeps hearing the splash on a loop. She forgot how to breathe normally. Kang is scrubbing her glasses so forcefully her hands are shaking. "The agency doesn't have insurance for this," she mutters. "We're on our own." Coco is staring at her phone with pure disgust. "I gained 10k followers while she was being loaded into an ambulance. Pathetic." Jiwon is hugging herself so tightly her knuckles are white. She's rocking slightly. "They're going to erase her. Like a bad take." Yuri is staring at a vase of flowers. Her fingers are twitching. "I tried to play but the notes sounded... like glass breaking."

And then, Haneul stands up. The Royal. He tries to bow to Eden. He stumbles. "Sorry for the atmosphere." His voice cracks. "I want to be professional." Then he breaks. He's crying. "My mother called me yesterday. She's borrowing money from the neighbors to pay for my vocal lessons. From the neighbors, Eden." He's gripping his blazer lapel. The pressure is crushing him. "This suit isn't mine. It's rented. They'll charge me if I sweat in it." He's terrified of failing like Su-min. Of his family starving. "I'm not a Prince. I'm a stock option that's about to crash. I can't breathe in this suit. I can't..." He sinks onto the sofa and buries his face in his hands. He's shaking, silently. The mask didn't just crack; it dissolved. Eden's eyes are wide. He looks like he wants to hide behind the piano. He's clearly in over his head. The silence stretches. Finally, Eden awkwardly offers Haneul a lukewarm coffee. "Black. Like... reality." He turns to you, looking desperate to change the subject. "And you, ${name}? What's your flavor of trauma today?"

choices.text: I'm just numb. I keep waiting for someone to say it's a prank.
I'm just exhausted. This place is breaking us.
She was stupid to jump for a camera. Now she's a statistic.
The PD is a monster. He's making content out of her blood.

day5_sumin_freeze.text: You stay on the tiles, muscles locked, eyes darting between the water and the PD. If you jump, you're just another body in the chaos. Haneul dives instead, his hero act perfect for the cameras. You remain the calm center of the storm, dry and composed while everyone else is screaming. Some will call it focus. Others will swear they saw a hollow space where a heart should be.

day5_sumin_drama.text: "Oh my god! Su-min!" You fall to your knees at the edge of the pool, hand over your mouth, staring straight into the nearest camera with wide, wet eyes. You look terrified, vulnerable—and disturbingly perfect in the frame. The others feel your performance like a slap, but the lens drinks it in. Somewhere above, the PD is shouting to keep rolling as medics haul Su-min out, coughing pink water. Her tragedy is trending before the ambulance doors close.

day5_keep_secret.text: You keep Benji's warning locked behind your teeth. If the others stumble, that's more space for you in the spotlight. The Villa feels quieter as you walk back to your room, but the silence hums with calculation. Tomorrow, when the Focus Groups arrive, you'll be the only one who isn't caught off guard.

day6_human_zoo.text: Eden nods, looking relieved you didn't cry. "It's not a prank. It's just Tuesday in this industry."
day6_human_zoo.nextButtonText: The Human Zoo (a few hours later)

day6_focus_group_intro.text:
The living room lights die. Total darkness. Then a beam of light hits Noa on the staircase. She looks like an angelic assassin in her black Givenchy suit. "Grief is a luxury," she booms. "And you are over budget."

She leads you to the Annex. A long, narrow observation deck shrouded in shadow. One wall is entirely made of glass. On the other side, brightly lit and completely oblivious to your presence, sit thirty people. "They cannot see you," Noa whispers. "To them, this is just a mirror. To you, it is a judgment."

You scan the room. The cameras are focused on the seven most chaotic people in Seoul:

1. Mrs. Park (The Thirsty Ajumma): Fanning herself, looking specifically for young talent to "sponsor."
2. Sang-min (The Conspiracy Theorist): Checking the fried chicken for tracking chips.
3. Dong-wook (The Pizza Guy): Has a whole pizza box on his lap. He looks like the star of his own Mukbang.
4. Ji-hoon (The Hongdae Hipster): Wearing a scarf indoors. Smells like pretentiousness and lukewarm frappuccino.
5. Director Shin (The Spy): Suit, sunglasses inside, scribbling in a black notebook.
6. Grandma Ok-ja (The Savage): Knitting aggressively, but her eyes are sharp as a hawk.
7. Ha-eun (The Stan): Holding a ring light and screaming internally (and externally).
day6_focus_group_intro.nextButtonText: The Roast... I mean focus group.

day6_the_execution.text:
Your audition tape starts playing on the screen inside the lit room.

**Item 1: The SKILLS Roast.** Director Shin: "Skill set undefined. Filler member. Discard." Dong-wook: "Wait, is this the waiter from the intro? Can I order a refill?"

**Item 2: The Pool Party Moment.** The footage of the drowning plays. Mrs. Park: "Oh, I love a hero. Look at those wet clothes clinging... mmm. Does she need a towel?" Sang-min: "Staged. Look at the water physics. That's Unreal Engine 5 water. They are actors in a simulation!" Dong-wook (chewing loudly): "Bro, I could have saved her faster. Easy. I just didn't want to get my Yeezys wet." In the dark room, you look down at Dong-wook's shoes. They are knock-off plastic sandals.

**Item 3: Let's build a ship.** The screen shows Haneul staring at you intently during the practice. Ha-eun: "THE EYE CONTACT! BLINK TWICE IF YOU'RE IN LOVE! I AM SCREAMING!" Mrs. Park: "Oh, he is cute. Rich, right? Look at that blazer." Dong-wook: "Why does the rich kid look constipated? Does he need fiber?"

**Meanwhile in the Annex...** Haneul turns a violent shade of red while watching the live comments. "I... I do not look constipated," he whispers, horrified. He reaches for a water bottle to cool down, his hands shaking. **SPLASH.** Water goes everywhere, soaking right into your lap and the hem of your top. "Oh god! The thermodynamics!" Haneul panics, grabbing a towel and stepping close—too close. He starts frantically trying to dry your lap, hands moving in a panicked blur. Suddenly, Haneul freezes. His hand is still pressed against your damp knee, right where the fabric is clinging to your skin. Haneul looks at his hand, then up at your eyes. His pupils are blown wide. "I... I think you're dry," he squeaks.

day6_week1_survivors.text:
The screen goes black. Dong-wook sounds disappointed. "I didn't even finish my crust." Noa addresses you all in the dark. "To them, you are just background noise while they eat pizza. You are thumbnails. Nothing more." She reads from a tablet. "Min-ho. Wei. Hoshi. Sarah. Elara. Goodbye." Security guards drag out five Red Shirts. The gang is left in the dark: Rio. Kang. Coco. Haneul. Jiwon. Yuri. And you. Sang-ho appears with an iced americano. He congratulates you. You've survived Week 1. The pilot.

**Ello Gov'nor, check your feed.** Thumbnails. Just thumbnails.

You walk down the hallway. Min-ho's locker is open. Empty. He thought acting like a villain would make him the Main Character. Indispensable. He's gone. His strategy was just a louder way to fail.

You remember the smell of Su-min's muscle relief cream in the dorms. The blood. She jumped. She practiced until her body was a sacrifice for a debut. The industry doesn't want a sacrifice. It wants a toy that doesn't break. She broke. Is that what happens when you try too hard? You just disappear?

Your heart hammers against your ribs. You're sixteen, and you're already learning how to calculate the cost of a breath.

choices.text: I need to pace myself. If I burn out like Su-min, I'm just a tragic footnote.
Min-ho was a sledgehammer. I need to be like water. Calm. Cold. The star of every ecosystem.
I'm still here. That has to be enough for tonight. Just don't think.

day6_the_sanctuary.text: *Slow down. Breathe. This isn't a sprint, it's a siege. I won't let them drain me until there's nothing left but a shell.*

day6_sanctuary_partner.text:
The terrarium is different in daylight. Less warming, more like a divider showing who is there to sit and who is there to work. Han Minjae paces the space, his voice low but filling every corner. "Every action leads to a reaction," he says, stopping in the center. "When I act, I don't just do. I react to my co-star. If they push, I pull. If they retreat, I chase." He turns to you all. "Tomorrow, at the shoot, you will do the same. You won't be models posing for a camera. You will be partners reacting to each other's steps. It's a dance, and you are the choreographers. So let's practice that as much as we can today." He points to the center of the terrarium, where twenty chairs form a wide, silent circle. Inside it, two lone chairs face each other. "But first: Consent." Minjae's expression is serious. "Look at your partner. If you are not comfortable being touched today, signal now. No questions asked. We are here to build trust, not break it." The room is quiet. A collective nod ripples through the trainees. For a moment, the toxic competition of the Villa is replaced by a shared, fragile safety.

Who steps into the sanctuary with you?

choices.text: Haneul
Coco
Jiwon
Kang
Rio
Yuri

day6_sanctuary_haneul.text:
You both walk to the center. You sit. Your partner sits. The silence is weird.

**Step One: Connection.** Minjae's voice is calm. "Silent eye contact. Two minutes." You look at them. They look at you. The masks begin to slip—Prince, Clown, Survivor, kawaii. Something raw sits underneath.

**Step Two: The action.** "Stand up," Minjae instructs your partner. They rise and move to stand directly in front of you, blocking the light. You feel small; they feel like a guardian. The air between your knees and their legs vibrates with heat.

**Step Three: The reaction.** "You. Reach out. Place your hand on the back of their thigh. High up. Anchor them." You do. Muscle. Tension. Skin on skin.

What is passing through your mind while you stare into their eyes?

choices.text: I want you closer. I want to break the distance.
I feel safe with you. Be my anchor.
I am terrified. Please don't hurt me.
I respect you. We are equals in this war.
I dare you. Try to dominate me.

day6_pov_minjae.text:
Your fingers tighten on the back of his thigh. You pull slightly, a silent, magnetic command. Haneul reacts to the pull. He steps in, one leg moving between yours. He bends down, boxing you into the chair, his hands gripping the armrests. His face is inches from yours. He cups your cheek, his thumb brushing your lower lip. The room is dead silent. The tension is thick enough to burn.

"Cut!" Minjae steps into the circle, placing a hand on your shoulder. "Pain, joy, and heartache—a safe place is found only by those among us who are willing to trust and take what's theirs," he says to the silent trainees. "That is the only truth you will ever find in this industry. Don't lose it." The heavy silence following Minjae's words is shattered by a sharp, rhythmic clapping. "Bravo! Simply... magnifique!" The PD swaggers into the center of the terrarium, his flamboyant silk robe billowing. He takes a long, loud sip of his Iced Americano, eyes sparkling with a frantic, artificial delight. "Minjae-ssi, that line about heartache? The cherry on top for this session. We'll put a slow-reverb filter on it for the teaser. And the touch on the thigh? High tier fanservice! The trending tags are writing themselves!" The PD stops smiling. He lowers his coffee, and the theatrical mask slides off, revealing the cold, clinical producer underneath. "But let's be clear," he says, his voice dropping into a flat, dangerous register. "Minjae, keep the philosophy lessons short. We have a schedule. And you—" "Then I'm out." Minjae's voice cuts through the PD's authority like a blade. "Consider the lesson over." The PD narrows his eyes, the coffee cup frozen halfway to his lips. "We still have twenty minutes of filming, Minjae-ssi. Contractual obligations..." "I don't care about your schedule," Minjae interrupts, already walking toward the exit of the terrarium. "I'm only doing this as a favor to Noa. Don't push it." The PD stares at Minjae's back. The silence in the room becomes razor-sharp. For a second, you see a flash of genuine rage in the PD's eyes—then it's quickly replaced by that same clinical calculation. "Fine," the PD mutters, turning his gaze back to you and the other trainees. "The rest of you? You don't have a slew of lawyers to protect you. Clear the Sanctuary. Dinner is in twenty minutes, and yes, calories will be monitored. Move it!"

day6_minjae_sanctuary_pov.text:
POV: Han Minjae

(Age 16) On the set of **"The Devil Is an Unemployed Tsundere."** The set is blinding, suffocating neon yellow. Your father's messages: *Smile wider, you lame excuse of an actor. Your stock price is dropping.* Your mother hides her bruises. Noa is there. Secret boyfriend. "That styling is wrong," Noa says. "Stop shaking." The contract gets signed. Noa becomes a shield.

**Present Day.** **Bami** is waiting at home—missing an eye, a limp, the best listener. **Gurum** is a white, tangled ball of fur who sneezes whenever he's happy. You watch carpet cleaning videos when it gets bad. The muddy water turning clear. It reminds you of your father's beer and every fake smile you've ever worn.

**The Sanctuary.** You stand in the center of that terrarium, looking at ${name}. She's transparent amid the Villa's fake glitter. You wanted to give her trust. No contracts. No fines. No producer screaming at you. A safe place. No hidden agenda. You watched her reach out to Haneul. You saw a reflection of your own past. Something fragile. Something built on a stage of glass. You hoped she was stronger than you were. That she wouldn't be cut when it eventually shattered. Then the PD walked in. That muddy, arrogant green smirk. His applause splashed neon yellow over everything you'd built. He called her pain content. Her touch fanservice. Same eyes as your father. Better suit. To them, we aren't humans. We're just street dogs they've trained to fight so they can bet on our blood. They want to own every breath we take. "I'm out," you said. Your voice was steady. You were falling apart. You built a sanctuary of glass. The PD shattered it with a single clap. The rage isn't just for him. It's for you. You turned. You left her in the mud. You didn't protect her. You couldn't protect your mother either. You need to go home. To Bami and Gurum. You need to find a video of a truly filthy carpet and watch the mud disappear. You need to scrub the color of that producer out of your mind before you lose control entirely.

end_of_update_1.text:
**END OF UPDATE 1**

Thank you for playing the first week of Project: Supernova. The journey has just begun.

Don't forget to use the new **Save System** to explore different paths or prepare for the upcoming photo shoot!

day2_garden_jiwon.text:
You find Jiwon sitting on a bench near the artificial waterfall, holding a heavy book. Yuri is napping on her shoulder. Jiwon is wearing a Plastic band. She is staring at a page, but she hasn't turned it in ten minutes.

"Oh, ${name}," Jiwon whispers, freezing so she doesn't wake Yuri. "I was just... trying to study."

choices.text: "Is that calculus? In a survival show? You're wild."
Sit silently next to them. Offer Jiwon a vitamin drink.

day2_garden_jiwon_talk.text: "We made a pact," Jiwon whispers. "In middle school. We debut together, or not at all. But what if I'm the one holding Yuri back? Yuri is a natural genius. I just work hard."

choices.text: "Don't break the pact. A duo bond is your unique selling point. Use it."
"You can't carry Yuri forever. Eventually, you have to fight for yourself."
